seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Smolagents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Smolagents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Smolagents enables lightweight, code-based reasoning for real-time conversational AI, processing speech-to-text inputs through simple agent loops. However, the high-stakes nature of voice interactions—demanding sub-second latency and safety—amplifies risks like unsafe code generation and coherence failures. Vex guardrails ensure reliability by verifying responses, latency, and safety in this critical pipeline.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Smolagents
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents follow a real-time pipeline: microphone audio streams to a speech-to-text model (e.g., Whisper Tiny), producing text fed into a Smolagents loop for reasoning and tool calls. The agent generates responses via code agents or tools, which are converted to speech using a fast TTS model like Piper or Bark Nano, targeting <500ms end-to-end latency. Smolagents' simplicity shines here, but lacks native error handling, making Vex essential for wrapping agent runs.

The core loop is: STT → Smolagent.run() → TTS, with state management for conversation history. Multi-step reasoning in Smolagents can compound errors, especially under latency pressure, where agents might generate inefficient or unsafe code.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Mic → [STT: Whisper] → [Smolagents Loop: Code/Tools] → [TTS: Piper] → Speaker
                  ↑                           ↓
             Vex.verify() ← Latency/Safety Checks → Conversation State

risks.name: Unsafe Python Execution
risks.description: Smolagents code agents can generate exec() calls with user-controlled inputs, enabling RCE in voice pipelines.
risks.mitigation: Wrap agent.run() with Vex verify() using code_safety checks

risks.name: Latency Budget Violations
risks.description: Multi-step reasoning exceeds sub-second requirements, causing choppy voice interactions.

risks.name: Conversation Incoherence
risks.description: Agents lose context across turns, generating irrelevant or repetitive responses.
risks.mitigation: Vex coherence checks against conversation history

risks.name: Unfiltered Toxic Responses
risks.description: Voice agents amplify harmful content through TTS without safety layers.
risks.mitigation: Vex response appropriateness and safety filtering

implementation.title: Basic Smolagents Voice Loop
implementation.description: Core agent setup with STT/TTS integration for real-time processing.
implementation.code:
from smolagents import CodeAgent, tool
from faster_whisper import WhisperModel
import pyttsx3

stt = WhisperModel("tiny")
tts = pyttsx3.init()

@tool
def speak(text: str):
    tts.say(text)
    tts.runAndWait()

agent = CodeAgent(tools=[speak], model="gpt-4o-mini")

def voice_loop(audio_input):
    text = stt.transcribe(audio_input)[0].text
    response = agent.run(text)
    speak(response)

implementation.title: Streaming STT Integration
implementation.description: Real-time audio chunking for sub-second transcription latency.
implementation.code:
import pyaudio
import numpy as np

CHUNK = 1024
FORMAT = pyaudio.paInt16
CHANNELS = 1
RATE = 16000

p = pyaudio.PyAudio()
stream = p.open(format=FORMAT, channels=CHANNELS, rate=RATE, input=True, frames_per_buffer=CHUNK)

def get_realtime_transcript():
    frames = []
    for _ in range(0, int(RATE / CHUNK * 3)):  # 3s buffer
        data = stream.read(CHUNK)
        frames.append(np.frombuffer(data, dtype=np.int16))
    audio = np.concatenate(frames)
    segments, _ = stt.transcribe(audio, beam_size=5)
    return ' '.join(seg.text for seg in segments)

implementation.title: Conversation State Management
implementation.description: Persistent memory for multi-turn voice coherence.
implementation.code:
history = []

def voice_agent_with_memory(user_input):
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": user_input})
    context = "\n".join([f"{msg['role']}: {msg['content']}" for msg in history[-8:]])
    response = agent.run(f"Context: {context}\nRespond naturally:")
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response})
    return response

implementation.title: Low-Latency TTS Pipeline
implementation.description: Fast TTS optimized for real-time voice output.
implementation.code:
from piper import PiperVoice

voice = PiperVoice.load("en_US-lessac-medium.onnx")

def fast_tts(text):
    with open("output.wav", "wb") as f:
        voice.synthesize(text, f)
    # Play with low-latency audio
    import pygame
    pygame.mixer.init(frequency=22050)
    pygame.mixer.music.load("output.wav")
    pygame.mixer.music.play()
    while pygame.mixer.music.get_busy():
        pygame.time.wait(10)
